title: Announcing Google Antigravity SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first SDK that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It integrates agents with code editors, terminals, and browsers to automate software development tasks.
writeup: A short promotional introduction to the Google Anti-Gravity SDK, which exposes the agent engine behind the Anti-Gravity product as a Python library for custom projects. The SDK centers on a policy engine that governs when and how agents invoke tools, alongside built-in triggers for proactive, event-driven behavior. It ships with MCP support, skills, custom tools, and hooks aimed at Google-scale infrastructure from the start.
key_points: The Anti-Gravity SDK packages the same agent engine that powers the Anti-Gravity product into a Python library, letting developers spin up interactive agents with a minimal script.
A policy engine controls tool invocation conditions: agents can be restricted to specific argument values or run in an unrestricted 'YOLO' mode depending on the policy set.
Built-in triggers allow agents to monitor background events and proactively surface notifications when attention is required, rather than waiting for user prompts.
Out-of-the-box support includes MCP, skills, custom tools, and hooks, all designed to run on Google-scale infrastructure without additional boilerplate.
